script:
Ninety seconds. Here is the day.

The market. Databricks is now in our business: CustomerLake puts agentic identity resolution inside the lakehouse, with HP and Circle K already in preview. Snowflake spelled out native identity matching in the same stretch it committed six billion dollars to AWS for agent compute. Read together, the platforms are building identity down while the money moves to agents. And Hightouch shipped agentic campaign tooling promptable from Claude, so the composable side now sells execution, not pipes.

The house. Identity is the substrate holds at 72, but it took its hardest week since we seeded it. CustomerLake is a heavy challenge and it is corroborated. The counterweight is real: Snowflake's own report ranks us the identity leader in its ecosystem. One watch item moved: identity could commoditize ticked up again. That is the belief the next two quarters will argue about.

The week. Four of five moves are on track. Derek's coexistence brief is agreed and moving. Retention is the exception: the war room move is still only proposed, and that priority has missed two weeks running. Amy owns the unblock, and the blocker is named: usage data for six accounts still sits in the old warehouse.

The call. Decide the public posture on CustomerLake today. Silence reads as surprise, and the coexistence line is already drafted. Kabir owns it, due tomorrow.
